title: Chapter Twenty-Eight: Legacy
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The bell shattered the stillness.

Not the clear, measured tone of a monastery's call to prayer. This was urgent -- rapid strikes hammered against bronze, the sound rolling down the mountain like a warning cry.

The faces of every Zhongnan member present changed in an instant.

"Tyrant Sword Sect!"

"They've come again."

Wei Ji Myo-hwa's head snapped toward the mountain gate. The Zhongnan Sword Immortal had already moved -- one moment beside them, the next a blur of grey robes disappearing down the slope with a speed that made Won-chang's jaw drop.

"Disciple Jeong, take care of Hye," he called without turning.

"Have the guests wait here," Wei Ji Myo-hwa added, and then she was gone. Only the flutter of her hair trailing behind her was visible before the trees swallowed her.

Yeon-shin turned to his brother.

"This doesn't seem like it's happened just once or twice."

"We never know where or how they'll clash," Jung-san said, his expression dark. "The main masters often cross paths and strike. Like now."

*The Zhongnan Sword Immortal is guarding the main mountain. That reveals how thin their numbers are.*

"I've seen the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader several times," Jung-san said. He wiped his forehead. "Even when he invaded our sect after massacring a family, he was leisurely... at that time, my heart..."

The immature brother of the Jeong family was gone. In his place stood a Zhongnan disciple full of worry.

Won-chang struck the ground. "Misfortune the moment we arrive."

"This is when Desolate should step in," Yeon-shin said.

"Even if Sir Jeong says so -- a Nine Sects martial artist is nothing without his pride. Didn't we just experience that at the gate? If the Sword Dragon hadn't been there--"

"It doesn't look that leisurely."

The cheers were rising. The front line was being pushed back. Through the trees, the clash of metal on metal carried up the mountainside, sharp and incessant.

Five figures appeared on the Seeking Pavilion's roof.

Zhongnan martial artists. They had been hidden there -- protecting Hye, or the pavilion, or both. Their energy was clear, righteous, distinctly different from the sinister qi of the Thirteen Heavens.

"Do not carelessly comment on our sect's seniors," one of them said.

They had heard him analyzing the battle. And they did not like what they had heard.

"I don't think my words were disrespectful," Yeon-shin said.

"Words approach differently depending on the person. Desolate Sect cannot become a guest of the martial world."

*Desolate Sect has many enemies.*

But there was no time for politics.

Five more figures approached from the east. Orange martial uniforms. Swords drawn. Energy that made the five Zhongnan guards look like children holding sticks.

Tyrant Sword Sect.

They had bypassed the main battle. Infiltrated deep into the mountain. And they were heading straight for Hye.

"Kill the child first," their leader said. His voice was flat, indifferent. "She's continuing the Sword Immortal's lineage."

The four beside him launched without a word. Their footfalls cracked the stone beneath them.

"How dare you!"

The Zhongnan guards leapt from the roof. Steel met steel in the air -- the impact blasting leaves from the trees, whipping hair and robes with a violent wind.

Four against five. Evenly matched. But the Tyrant Sword Sect leader walked through the melee untouched. The Zhongnan guards could not spare a blade for him.

He locked eyes with Yeon-shin. Hye was hidden behind Yeon-shin's back.

"Desolate Sect youngsters," the man said with a smile. "Answer why you've come to the Nine Sects. To balance the situation since our sect is dominant?"

Yeon-shin looked at Won-chang.

"Please assist me."

"Don't worry about the back."

Won-chang's smile was thin and sharp. A man who had entered a life-or-death situation and found, to his own surprise, that he was eager for it.

The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique roared through Yeon-shin's body. Heavy energy gathered in his legs. Dust rose from the ground around his feet.

He stamped.

The sound was like a war drum. The stone beneath his foot fractured. The blast of displaced air and dust rolled outward in every direction, obscuring the battlefield for a heartbeat.

When it cleared, Yeon-shin was already inside the leader's guard.

The Desolate Sword flashed white. The Eternal Blossom Fist's Advancing Thunder stance surged from his left fist simultaneously -- a wave of compressed energy that pushed the air itself aside.

The Tyrant Sword martial artist barely blocked. The impact drove him backward, his heels gouging furrows in the stone. Two of his companions broke from the Zhongnan guards and leapt toward Yeon-shin.

Won-chang intercepted them.

His reversed sword flashed through the gap between the two attackers -- the initial stance of Radiant Demon technique deflecting the first sword, his return stroke catching the second man just below the breastbone. Won-chang in true combat was a different creature entirely. No banter. No fidgeting. Just clean, economical killing with a smile at the corner of his mouth.

Yeon-shin pressed forward. The Radiant Demon swift sword technique poured from his hand like water -- strike after strike, giving no room to breathe. When the leader's counter-thrust came for his throat, Yeon-shin slipped it with Radiant Wing Step and drove the Desolate Sword through the opening.

Through the heart.

The man's lips trembled. "This... with a sword without depth..."

Yeon-shin pulled the blade free in a single motion. Blood arced. Time froze across the peak as every fighter stopped to stare.

"I know it has no depth," Yeon-shin said.

He shook the blood from his sword.

Won-chang had already dealt with the rest. Four Tyrant Sword bodies lay in various states of ruin among the pavilion's broken stones.

The Zhongnan guards stared. Their earlier pride and jealousy had been replaced by something simpler and harder to deny.

Awe.

"We reacted too sensitively," one of them said. "We apologize for our rudeness."

"Radiant Demon Wing's Jeong Yeon-shin," Won-chang announced, chest out, eyes bright. "You won't forget. It'll be a name that shakes the martial world soon enough."

---

The Zhongnan Sword Immortal and Wei Ji Myo-hwa returned after the main battle settled.

"Zhongnan owes you," Jong Yeo-il said.

"I protected my blood relative."

The old man shook his head. "Hye will become a pillar of our sect, following Myo-hwa. Even if I say you did what was naturally expected as an uncle, our sect is indeed grateful."

"Then, if you'll excuse me," Yeon-shin said. His voice had changed -- taut, focused, a new urgency humming beneath the calm. "May I train here? The sensation I've awakened is gripping my mind and won't let go."

"There's an insight!" The Sword Immortal's eyes widened. He waved everyone back immediately.

Yeon-shin glanced once more at the Sword Master's cliff. Drew the Desolate Sword.

*Each real battle is new.*

Sunlight streamed in lines along the pure white blade. He extended it beneath the sword wave he held low and began to move.

The first trajectory was smooth. No special energy -- just the sword following his arm in a line that seemed to gather light along its edge. As if the sun fell a little closer to the steel.

The Zhongnan Sword Immortal's eyes grew wide.

But the Desolate Sword could not progress further. Yeon-shin's breath roughened. The intention was there -- brilliant, burning at the crown of his skull -- but the true energy would not rise as his mind demanded. The sword traces on the cliff did not offer a technique to copy. They offered a direction.

*Find your own sword.*

He lowered the blade.

"I will not take the Sword Master's legacy," he said.

Silence. Then he continued, choosing his words slowly, as if pulling them from deep water.

"It's as if the traces say -- there is this sword path, so broaden your thoughts. That's how it felt."

The Sword Immortal nodded as if understanding what no one else could.

Beside him, Wei Ji Myo-hwa's expression was hard to read.

"Traces of a great master who has achieved mastery contain spiritual energy," Jong Yeo-il said. "It transcends the boundaries of martial arts and techniques. You've resonated with it. Heavenly luck and talent have met."

Something shifted in Yeon-shin's understanding. A door opening -- not the grief-door, but another one. The Sword Master had spent his later years in Zhongnan, exchanging insights with Zhongnan masters. The sword techniques born here were thick with Zhongnan's colour -- heavy, deep-rooted. They would not suit his body.

But the *principle* behind them...

"Wait," he told Wei Ji Myo-hwa, who had been opening her mouth to speak. "My training is not over."

His consciousness sank into the Desolate Sword.

The experience came like heaven and earth crushing into a single line. If merely striking faster than the opponent had been sufficient, he now understood why techniques like Taiji Wisdom Sword and Plum Blossom Sword were renowned across the world. A true sword technique was not mere speed. It was intention melted into true energy, creating a harmony that had never before existed.

The sword's name resonated in his hand.

The Desolate Sword, wrapped in falling sunlight, rose toward the sky. The blade caught the light and held it -- truly held it, the steel becoming a ribbon of captured radiance. Then it fell, tracing a pure white arc that separated heaven from earth.

A sword dance. The Sword Master's legacy blooming in a young man's hands -- not as imitation, but as a bridge to something new.

Tears ran down the Zhongnan Sword Immortal's wrinkled face.

"Oh, Sword Master..."

---
